revision.text:
[[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354485]] Wikidata name: three-headed lion dance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanbiki Shishimai''' (三匹獅子舞) is a form of [[:en:Lion dance|Lion dance]] performed by a trio of individuals, widely distributed throughout [[Eastern Japan]], particularly in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]. It is also referred to as ''Hitoridachi Sanbiki Shishimai'' or ''Santō Shishimai''. The performance is accompanied by [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]] (bamboo flutes) and [[:en:Sasara|Sasara]] (percussion instruments), while the performers play [[:en:Taiko|Taiko]] drums tied to their waists as they dance. Some performances include additional roles such as [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], [[:en:Kappa|Kappa]], [[:en:monkeys|monkeys]], ''tayū'' (masters), [[Shinto priests]], or even [[:en:nakadachi|nakadachi]] (intermediaries) serving as comedic characters. Primarily conducted during [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] [[:en:Festivals|Festivals]], the dances are offerings for [[bountiful harvests]], [[:en:disaster prevention|disaster prevention]], [[rain prayers]], and gratitude. Locals often refer to the dance as "shishi," "shishimai," "sasara shishimai," or simply "sasara." This style of lion dance belongs to the ''fūryū'' (aesthetic) tradition, which developed during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]] and [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], distinct from [[gagaku]]-based lion dances of [[:en:Ancient history|Ancient history]] and [[Kagura]] origin. {{右|[[Image:San002.JPG|thumb|240px|none|Togasaki Katori Shrine
Sanbiki Shishimai performance
(Photographed on July 6, 2003)]]}} == Current Preservation == Unlike large shrine festivals supported by urban [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (parishioners), most Sanbiki Shishimai performances are small-scale "village festivals" with limited recognition outside the local community. Many groups traditionally limited the transmission of the dance to the [[eldest sons]] of farming families, ensuring continuity by choosing individuals unlikely to leave their land. However, adherence to this practice in modern times has caused the dance to fade in some areas. Conversely, some groups now teach the tradition to local [[:en:elementary|elementary]] and [[:en:junior high school|junior high school]] students. == Performance Locations and Dates == === January === * {{ill|Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja|銚子口香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chōshiguchi Shishimai), designated as a [[Kasukabe City]] cultural property, performed on the Sunday closest to [[:en:January 15|January 15]] – ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[Kasukabe City]]) === March === * Aizu Higan Shishi (Komatsu Shishimai of Kita-Aizu), heralding spring in [[:en:Aizu|Aizu]] during the [[:en:Equinox|Equinox]]. Notably, during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], [[Yamakawa Ōkura]] of the [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu Domain]] led the dance to [[:en:Wakamatsu Castle|Wakamatsu Castle]] as it was under siege – ([[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu|Aizuwakamatsu]]) === April === * [[:en:Gwaneumsa|Kannonji (Ishihara Sasara Shishimai)]] – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Saitama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], held on the 3rd weekend of April – ([[Kawagoe City]], Saitama) * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} (Higashikogura Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Nikkō City]] cultural property, performed on [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]) – ([[Nikkō City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]) === May === * {{ill|Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|ja|鳥見神社 (印西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|1=Torimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675121}} (Torimi Shrine Shishimai), designated as a [[Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Chiba Prefecture cultural property]], performed on [[:en:May 3|May 3]] – ([[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[Inzai City]]) * Nagasaki Shrine (Shishimai of Nagasaki Shrine) – ([[Toshima City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]) === July === [[Image:Tabayama Sasara-Jishi2.JPG|240px|thumb|right|[[Tabayama Gion Festival]]
Sasara Shishimai performance
(Photographed July 10, 2010)]] * Ose Sengen and Hikawa Shrines (Ose Shishimai) – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], performed on the first weekend of July – ([[Yashio City]], Saitama) === August === * Yahata Shrine (Komukai Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Kanagawa Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Kanagawa Prefecture cultural property]], performed on the 2nd Sunday of August – ([[Saiwai Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) === September === * [[Kinza Hachimangū]] (Kinza Sasara Dance), designated as a [[Yokote City]] cultural property, performed on [[:en:September 15|September 15]] – ([[Yokote City]], [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]]) === October === [[Image:熊野神社ささら獅子舞.jpg|代替文=Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai|サムネイル|Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai]] * Sugao Shrine (Hatsuyama Shishimai), designated as a Kanagawa cultural property, performed on the 1st Sunday of October – ([[Miyamae Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://takamizusan-sisimai.o.oo7.jp/ High Water Mountain Classical Lion Dance] * [http://nippara-forest.com/shishimai/index.html Shimonaguri Suwa Shrine Shishimai] * [http://purespirits.biz/sasara/ Nagano Shishimai of Kyuizu Shrine, Gyōda City, Saitama Prefecture] {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三匹獅子舞|version=101924445|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese folk culture]] [[Category:Lions in art]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[Category:Performing arts in the Edo period]] [[Category:Japanese Lion Dance]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11354485}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

This style of lion dance belongs to the ''fūryū'' (aesthetic) tradition, which developed during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]] and [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], distinct from [[gagaku]]-based lion dances of [[:en:Ancient history|Ancient history]] and [[Kagura]] origin. {{右|[[Image:San002.JPG|thumb|240px|none|Togasaki Katori Shrine
Sanbiki Shishimai performance
(Photographed on July 6, 2003)]]}} == Current Preservation == Unlike large shrine festivals supported by urban [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (parishioners), most Sanbiki Shishimai performances are small-scale "village festivals" with limited recognition outside the local community. Many groups traditionally limited the transmission of the dance to the [[eldest sons]] of farming families, ensuring continuity by choosing individuals unlikely to leave their land. However, adherence to this practice in modern times has caused the dance to fade in some areas. Conversely, some groups now teach the tradition to local [[:en:elementary|elementary]] and [[:en:junior high school|junior high school]] students. == Performance Locations and Dates == === January === * {{ill|Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja|銚子口香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chōshiguchi Shishimai), designated as a [[Kasukabe City]] cultural property, performed on the Sunday closest to [[:en:January 15|January 15]] – ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[Kasukabe City]]) === March === * Aizu Higan Shishi (Komatsu Shishimai of Kita-Aizu), heralding spring in [[:en:Aizu|Aizu]] during the [[:en:Equinox|Equinox]]. Notably, during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], [[Yamakawa Ōkura]] of the [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu Domain]] led the dance to [[:en:Wakamatsu Castle|Wakamatsu Castle]] as it was under siege – ([[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu|Aizuwakamatsu]]) === April === * [[:en:Gwaneumsa|Kannonji (Ishihara Sasara Shishimai)]] – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Saitama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], held on the 3rd weekend of April – ([[Kawagoe City]], Saitama) * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} (Higashikogura Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Nikkō City]] cultural property, performed on [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]) – ([[Nikkō City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]) === May === * {{ill|Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|ja|鳥見神社 (印西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|1=Torimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675121}} (Torimi Shrine Shishimai), designated as a [[Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Chiba Prefecture cultural property]], performed on [[:en:May 3|May 3]] – ([[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[Inzai City]]) * Nagasaki Shrine (Shishimai of Nagasaki Shrine) – ([[Toshima City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]) === July === [[Image:Tabayama Sasara-Jishi2.JPG|240px|thumb|right|[[Tabayama Gion Festival]]
Sasara Shishimai performance
(Photographed July 10, 2010)]] * Ose Sengen and Hikawa Shrines (Ose Shishimai) – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], performed on the first weekend of July – ([[Yashio City]], Saitama) === August === * Yahata Shrine (Komukai Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Kanagawa Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Kanagawa Prefecture cultural property]], performed on the 2nd Sunday of August – ([[Saiwai Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) === September === * [[Kinza Hachimangū]] (Kinza Sasara Dance), designated as a [[Yokote City]] cultural property, performed on [[:en:September 15|September 15]] – ([[Yokote City]], [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]]) === October === [[Image:熊野神社ささら獅子舞.jpg|代替文=Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai|サムネイル|Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai]] * Sugao Shrine (Hatsuyama Shishimai), designated as a Kanagawa cultural property, performed on the 1st Sunday of October – ([[Miyamae Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://takamizusan-sisimai.o.oo7.jp/ High Water Mountain Classical Lion Dance] * [http://nippara-forest.com/shishimai/index.html Shimonaguri Suwa Shrine Shishimai] * [http://purespirits.biz/sasara/ Nagano Shishimai of Kyuizu Shrine, Gyōda City, Saitama Prefecture] {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三匹獅子舞|version=101924445|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] {{wikidata link|Q11354485}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese folk culture]] [[Category:Japanese Lion Dance]] [[Category:Lions in art]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Performing arts in the Edo period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354485]] Wikidata name: three-headed lion dance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanbiki Shishimai''' (三匹獅子舞) is a form of [[:en:Lion dance|Lion dance]] performed by a trio of individuals, widely distributed throughout [[Eastern Japan]], particularly in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]. It is also referred to as ''Hitoridachi Sanbiki Shishimai'' or ''Santō Shishimai''. The performance is accompanied by [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]] (bamboo flutes) and [[:en:Sasara|Sasara]] (percussion instruments), while the performers play [[:en:Taiko|Taiko]] drums tied to their waists as they dance. Some performances include additional roles such as [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], [[:en:Kappa|Kappa]], [[:en:monkeys|monkeys]], ''tayū'' (masters), [[Shinto priests]], or even [[:en:nakadachi|nakadachi]] (intermediaries) serving as comedic characters. Primarily conducted during [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] [[:en:Festivals|Festivals]], the dances are offerings for [[bountiful harvests]], [[:en:disaster prevention|disaster prevention]], [[rain prayers]], and gratitude. Locals often refer to the dance as "shishi," "shishimai," "sasara shishimai," or simply "sasara." This style of lion dance belongs to the ''fūryū'' (aesthetic) tradition, which developed during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]] and [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], distinct from [[gagaku]]-based lion dances of [[:en:Ancient history|Ancient history]] and [[Kagura]] origin. {{右|[[Image:San002.JPG|thumb|240px|none|Togasaki Katori Shrine
Sanbiki Shishimai performance
(Photographed on July 6, 2003)]]}} == Current Preservation == Unlike large shrine festivals supported by urban [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (parishioners), most Sanbiki Shishimai performances are small-scale "village festivals" with limited recognition outside the local community. Many groups traditionally limited the transmission of the dance to the [[eldest sons]] of farming families, ensuring continuity by choosing individuals unlikely to leave their land. However, adherence to this practice in modern times has caused the dance to fade in some areas. Notably, during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], [[Yamakawa Ōkura]] of the [[:en:Aizu Domain|Aizu Domain]] led the dance to [[:en:Wakamatsu Castle|Wakamatsu Castle]] as it was under siege – ([[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu|Aizuwakamatsu]]) === April === * [[:en:Gwaneumsa|Kannonji (Ishihara Sasara Shishimai)]] – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Saitama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], held on the 3rd weekend of April – ([[Kawagoe City]], Saitama) * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} (Higashikogura Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Nikkō City]] cultural property, performed on [[:en:April 29|April 29]] ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]) – ([[Nikkō City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]) === May === * {{ill|Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|ja|鳥見神社 (印西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|1=Torimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675121}} (Torimi Shrine Shishimai), designated as a [[Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Chiba Prefecture cultural property]], performed on [[:en:May 3|May 3]] – ([[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[Inzai City]]) * Nagasaki Shrine (Shishimai of Nagasaki Shrine) – ([[Toshima City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]) === July === [[Image:Tabayama Sasara-Jishi2.JPG|240px|thumb|right|[[Tabayama Gion Festival]]

revision.text:
[[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354485]] Wikidata name: three-headed lion dance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanbiki Shishimai''' (三匹獅子舞) is a form of {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Lion dance}} performed by a trio of individuals, widely distributed throughout [[Eastern Japan]], particularly in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}. It is also referred to as ''Hitoridachi Sanbiki Shishimai'' or ''Santō Shishimai''. The performance is accompanied by {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue}} (bamboo flutes) and [[:en:Sasara|Sasara]] (percussion instruments), while the performers play {{ill|Taiko|en|Taiko|WD=Q221769|lt=Taiko}} drums tied to their waists as they dance. Some performances include additional roles such as {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, {{ill|Kappa|en|Kappa|WD=Q14401|lt=Kappa}}, [[:en:monkeys|monkeys]], ''tayū'' (masters), [[Shinto priests]], or even [[:en:nakadachi|nakadachi]] (intermediaries) serving as comedic characters. Primarily conducted during {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} [[:en:Festivals|Festivals]], the dances are offerings for [[bountiful harvests]], {{ill|disaster prevention|en|disaster prevention|WD=Q30899704|lt=disaster prevention}}, [[rain prayers]], and gratitude. Locals often refer to the dance as "shishi," "shishimai," "sasara shishimai," or simply "sasara." This style of lion dance belongs to the ''fūryū'' (aesthetic) tradition, which developed during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Middle Ages}} and {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, distinct from [[gagaku]]-based lion dances of {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient history}} and [[Kagura]] origin. {{右|[[Image:San002.JPG|thumb|240px|none|Togasaki Katori Shrine
Sanbiki Shishimai performance
(Photographed on July 6, 2003)]]}} == Current Preservation == Unlike large shrine festivals supported by urban {{ill|Ujiko|en|Ujiko|WD=Q125562175|lt=Ujiko}} (parishioners), most Sanbiki Shishimai performances are small-scale "village festivals" with limited recognition outside the local community. Many groups traditionally limited the transmission of the dance to the [[eldest sons]] of farming families, ensuring continuity by choosing individuals unlikely to leave their land. However, adherence to this practice in modern times has caused the dance to fade in some areas. Conversely, some groups now teach the tradition to local {{ill|elementary|en|elementary|WD=Q402137|lt=elementary}} and [[:en:junior high school|junior high school]] students. == Performance Locations and Dates == === January === * {{ill|Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja|銚子口香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chōshiguchi Shishimai), designated as a [[Kasukabe City]] cultural property, performed on the Sunday closest to {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15}} – ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} [[Kasukabe City]]) === March === * Aizu Higan Shishi (Komatsu Shishimai of Kita-Aizu), heralding spring in {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu}} during the {{ill|Equinox|en|Equinox|WD=Q1315|lt=Equinox}}. Notably, during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, [[Yamakawa Ōkura]] of the {{ill|Aizu Domain|en|Aizu Domain|WD=Q4389944|lt=Aizu Domain}} led the dance to [[:en:Wakamatsu Castle|Wakamatsu Castle]] as it was under siege – ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|en|Aizuwakamatsu|WD=Q237699|lt=Aizuwakamatsu}}) === April === * {{ill|Gwaneumsa|en|Gwaneumsa|WD=Q840756|lt=Kannonji (Ishihara Sasara Shishimai)}} – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Saitama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], held on the 3rd weekend of April – ([[Kawagoe City]], Saitama) * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} (Higashikogura Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Nikkō City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}} ({{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Shōwa Day}}) – ([[Nikkō City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}) === May === * {{ill|Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|ja|鳥見神社 (印西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|1=Torimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675121}} (Torimi Shrine Shishimai), designated as a [[Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Chiba Prefecture cultural property]], performed on {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3}} – ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, [[Inzai City]]) * Nagasaki Shrine (Shishimai of Nagasaki Shrine) – ([[Toshima City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}) === July === [[Image:Tabayama Sasara-Jishi2.JPG|240px|thumb|right|[[Tabayama Gion Festival]]
Sasara Shishimai performance
(Photographed July 10, 2010)]] * Ose Sengen and Hikawa Shrines (Ose Shishimai) – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], performed on the first weekend of July – ([[Yashio City]], Saitama) === August === * Yahata Shrine (Komukai Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Kanagawa Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Kanagawa Prefecture cultural property]], performed on the 2nd Sunday of August – ([[Saiwai Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) === September === * [[Kinza Hachimangū]] (Kinza Sasara Dance), designated as a [[Yokote City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15}} – ([[Yokote City]], {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture}}) === October === [[Image:熊野神社ささら獅子舞.jpg|代替文=Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai|サムネイル|Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai]] * Sugao Shrine (Hatsuyama Shishimai), designated as a Kanagawa cultural property, performed on the 1st Sunday of October – ([[Miyamae Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://takamizusan-sisimai.o.oo7.jp/ High Water Mountain Classical Lion Dance] * [http://nippara-forest.com/shishimai/index.html Shimonaguri Suwa Shrine Shishimai] * [http://purespirits.biz/sasara/ Nagano Shishimai of Kyuizu Shrine, Gyōda City, Saitama Prefecture] {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三匹獅子舞|version=101924445|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] {{wikidata link|Q11354485}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese folk culture]] [[Category:Japanese Lion Dance]] [[Category:Lions in art]] [[Category:Performing arts in the Edo period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] '''Sanbiki Shishimai''' (三匹獅子舞) is a form of {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Lion dance}} performed by a trio of individuals, widely distributed throughout [[Eastern Japan]], particularly in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}. It is also referred to as ''Hitoridachi Sanbiki Shishimai'' or ''Santō Shishimai''. The performance is accompanied by {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue}} (bamboo flutes) and [[:en:Sasara|Sasara]] (percussion instruments), while the performers play {{ill|Taiko|en|Taiko|WD=Q221769|lt=Taiko}} drums tied to their waists as they dance. Some performances include additional roles such as {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, {{ill|Kappa|en|Kappa|WD=Q14401|lt=Kappa}}, [[:en:monkeys|monkeys]], ''tayū'' (masters), [[Shinto priests]], or even [[:en:nakadachi|nakadachi]] (intermediaries) serving as comedic characters. Primarily conducted during {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} [[:en:Festivals|Festivals]], the dances are offerings for [[bountiful harvests]], {{ill|disaster prevention|en|disaster prevention|WD=Q30899704|lt=disaster prevention}}, [[rain prayers]], and gratitude. Locals often refer to the dance as "shishi," "shishimai," "sasara shishimai," or simply "sasara." This style of lion dance belongs to the ''fūryū'' (aesthetic) tradition, which developed during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Middle Ages}} and {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, distinct from [[gagaku]]-based lion dances of {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient history}} and [[Kagura]] origin. {{右|[[Image:San002.JPG|thumb|240px|none|Togasaki Katori Shrine
Sanbiki Shishimai performance
(Photographed on July 6, 2003)]]}} == Current Preservation == Unlike large shrine festivals supported by urban {{ill|Ujiko|en|Ujiko|WD=Q125562175|lt=Ujiko}} (parishioners), most Sanbiki Shishimai performances are small-scale "village festivals" with limited recognition outside the local community. Many groups traditionally limited the transmission of the dance to the [[eldest sons]] of farming families, ensuring continuity by choosing individuals unlikely to leave their land. However, adherence to this practice in modern times has caused the dance to fade in some areas. Conversely, some groups now teach the tradition to local {{ill|elementary|en|elementary|WD=Q402137|lt=elementary}} and [[:en:junior high school|junior high school]] students. == Performance Locations and Dates == === January === * {{ill|Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja|銚子口香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chōshiguchi Shishimai), designated as a [[Kasukabe City]] cultural property, performed on the Sunday closest to {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15}} – ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} [[Kasukabe City]]) === March === * Aizu Higan Shishi (Komatsu Shishimai of Kita-Aizu), heralding spring in {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu}} during the {{ill|Equinox|en|Equinox|WD=Q1315|lt=Equinox}}. Notably, during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, [[Yamakawa Ōkura]] of the {{ill|Aizu Domain|en|Aizu Domain|WD=Q4389944|lt=Aizu Domain}} led the dance to [[:en:Wakamatsu Castle|Wakamatsu Castle]] as it was under siege – ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|en|Aizuwakamatsu|WD=Q237699|lt=Aizuwakamatsu}}) === April === * {{ill|Gwaneumsa|en|Gwaneumsa|WD=Q840756|lt=Kannonji (Ishihara Sasara Shishimai)}} – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Saitama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], held on the 3rd weekend of April – ([[Kawagoe City]], Saitama) * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} (Higashikogura Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Nikkō City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}} ({{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Shōwa Day}}) – ([[Nikkō City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}) === May === * {{ill|Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|ja|鳥見神社 (印西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|1=Torimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675121}} (Torimi Shrine Shishimai), designated as a [[Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Chiba Prefecture cultural property]], performed on {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3}} – ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, [[Inzai City]]) * Nagasaki Shrine (Shishimai of Nagasaki Shrine) – ([[Toshima City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}) === July === [[Image:Tabayama Sasara-Jishi2.JPG|240px|thumb|right|[[Tabayama Gion Festival]]
Sasara Shishimai performance
(Photographed July 10, 2010)]] * Ose Sengen and Hikawa Shrines (Ose Shishimai) – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], performed on the first weekend of July – ([[Yashio City]], Saitama) === August === * Yahata Shrine (Komukai Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Kanagawa Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Kanagawa Prefecture cultural property]], performed on the 2nd Sunday of August – ([[Saiwai Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) === September === * [[Kinza Hachimangū]] (Kinza Sasara Dance), designated as a [[Yokote City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15}} – ([[Yokote City]], {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture}}) === October === [[Image:熊野神社ささら獅子舞.jpg|代替文=Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai|サムネイル|Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai]] * Sugao Shrine (Hatsuyama Shishimai), designated as a Kanagawa cultural property, performed on the 1st Sunday of October – ([[Miyamae Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://takamizusan-sisimai.o.oo7.jp/ High Water Mountain Classical Lion Dance] * [http://nippara-forest.com/shishimai/index.html Shimonaguri Suwa Shrine Shishimai] * [http://purespirits.biz/sasara/ Nagano Shishimai of Kyuizu Shrine, Gyōda City, Saitama Prefecture] {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三匹獅子舞|version=101924445|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] {{wikidata link|Q11354485}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese folk culture]] [[Category:Japanese Lion Dance]] [[Category:Lions in art]] [[Category:Performing arts in the Edo period]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] '''Sanbiki Shishimai''' (三匹獅子舞) is a form of {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Lion dance}} performed by a trio of individuals, widely distributed throughout [[Eastern Japan]], particularly in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}. It is also referred to as ''Hitoridachi Sanbiki Shishimai'' or ''Santō Shishimai''. The performance is accompanied by {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue}} (bamboo flutes) and [[:en:Sasara|Sasara]] (percussion instruments), while the performers play {{ill|Taiko|en|Taiko|WD=Q221769|lt=Taiko}} drums tied to their waists as they dance. Some performances include additional roles such as {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, {{ill|Kappa|en|Kappa|WD=Q14401|lt=Kappa}}, [[:en:monkeys|monkeys]], ''tayū'' (masters), [[Shinto priests]], or even [[:en:nakadachi|nakadachi]] (intermediaries) serving as comedic characters. Primarily conducted during {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} [[:en:Festivals|Festivals]], the dances are offerings for [[bountiful harvests]], {{ill|disaster prevention|en|disaster prevention|WD=Q30899704|lt=disaster prevention}}, [[rain prayers]], and gratitude. Locals often refer to the dance as "shishi," "shishimai," "sasara shishimai," or simply "sasara." This style of lion dance belongs to the ''fūryū'' (aesthetic) tradition, which developed during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Middle Ages}} and {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, distinct from [[gagaku]]-based lion dances of {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient history}} and [[Kagura]] origin. {{右|[[Image:San002.JPG|thumb|240px|none|Togasaki Katori Shrine
Sanbiki Shishimai performance
(Photographed on July 6, 2003)]]}} == Current Preservation == Unlike large shrine festivals supported by urban {{ill|Ujiko|en|Ujiko|WD=Q125562175|lt=Ujiko}} (parishioners), most Sanbiki Shishimai performances are small-scale "village festivals" with limited recognition outside the local community. Many groups traditionally limited the transmission of the dance to the [[eldest sons]] of farming families, ensuring continuity by choosing individuals unlikely to leave their land. However, adherence to this practice in modern times has caused the dance to fade in some areas. Conversely, some groups now teach the tradition to local {{ill|elementary|en|elementary|WD=Q402137|lt=elementary}} and [[:en:junior high school|junior high school]] students. == Performance Locations and Dates == === January === * {{ill|Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja|銚子口香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chōshiguchi Shishimai), designated as a [[Kasukabe City]] cultural property, performed on the Sunday closest to {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15}} – ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} [[Kasukabe City]]) === March === * Aizu Higan Shishi (Komatsu Shishimai of Kita-Aizu), heralding spring in {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu}} during the {{ill|Equinox|en|Equinox|WD=Q1315|lt=Equinox}}. Notably, during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, [[Yamakawa Ōkura]] of the {{ill|Aizu Domain|en|Aizu Domain|WD=Q4389944|lt=Aizu Domain}} led the dance to [[:en:Wakamatsu Castle|Wakamatsu Castle]] as it was under siege – ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|en|Aizuwakamatsu|WD=Q237699|lt=Aizuwakamatsu}}) === April === * {{ill|Gwaneumsa|en|Gwaneumsa|WD=Q840756|lt=Kannonji (Ishihara Sasara Shishimai)}} – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Saitama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], held on the 3rd weekend of April – ([[Kawagoe City]], Saitama) * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} (Higashikogura Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Nikkō City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}} ({{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Shōwa Day}}) – ([[Nikkō City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}) === May === * {{ill|Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|ja|鳥見神社 (印西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|1=Torimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675121}} (Torimi Shrine Shishimai), designated as a [[Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Chiba Prefecture cultural property]], performed on {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3}} – ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, [[Inzai City]]) * Nagasaki Shrine (Shishimai of Nagasaki Shrine) – ([[Toshima City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}) === July === [[Image:Tabayama Sasara-Jishi2.JPG|240px|thumb|right|[[Tabayama Gion Festival]]
Sasara Shishimai performance
(Photographed July 10, 2010)]] * Ose Sengen and Hikawa Shrines (Ose Shishimai) – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], performed on the first weekend of July – ([[Yashio City]], Saitama) === August === * Yahata Shrine (Komukai Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Kanagawa Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Kanagawa Prefecture cultural property]], performed on the 2nd Sunday of August – ([[Saiwai Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) === September === * [[Kinza Hachimangū]] (Kinza Sasara Dance), designated as a [[Yokote City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15}} – ([[Yokote City]], {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture}}) === October === [[Image:熊野神社ささら獅子舞.jpg|代替文=Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai|サムネイル|Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai]] * Sugao Shrine (Hatsuyama Shishimai), designated as a Kanagawa cultural property, performed on the 1st Sunday of October – ([[Miyamae Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://takamizusan-sisimai.o.oo7.jp/ High Water Mountain Classical Lion Dance] * [http://nippara-forest.com/shishimai/index.html Shimonaguri Suwa Shrine Shishimai] * [http://purespirits.biz/sasara/ Nagano Shishimai of Kyuizu Shrine, Gyōda City, Saitama Prefecture] {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三匹獅子舞|version=101924445|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] {{wikidata link|Q11354485}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese folk culture]] [[Category:Japanese Lion Dance]] [[Category:Lions in art]] [[Category:Performing arts in the Edo period]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Notably, during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, [[Yamakawa Ōkura]] of the {{ill|Aizu Domain|en|Aizu Domain|WD=Q4389944|lt=Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain}} led the dance to [[:en:Wakamatsu Castle|Wakamatsu Castle]] as it was under siege – ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|en|Aizuwakamatsu|WD=Q237699|lt=Aizuwakamatsu|1=Aizuwakamatsu}}) === April === * {{ill|Gwaneumsa|en|Gwaneumsa|WD=Q840756|lt=Kannonji (Ishihara Sasara Shishimai)|1=Gwaneumsa}} – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Saitama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], held on the 3rd weekend of April – ([[Kawagoe City]], Saitama) * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} (Higashikogura Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Nikkō City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}} ({{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Shōwa Day|1=Shōwa Day}}) – ([[Nikkō City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}) === May === * {{ill|Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|ja|鳥見神社 (印西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|1=Torimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675121}} (Torimi Shrine Shishimai), designated as a [[Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Chiba Prefecture cultural property]], performed on {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}} – ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, [[Inzai City]]) * Nagasaki Shrine (Shishimai of Nagasaki Shrine) – ([[Toshima City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}) === July === [[Image:Tabayama Sasara-Jishi2.JPG|240px|thumb|right|[[Tabayama Gion Festival]]
Sasara Shishimai performance
(Photographed July 10, 2010)]] * Ose Sengen and Hikawa Shrines (Ose Shishimai) – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], performed on the first weekend of July – ([[Yashio City]], Saitama) === August === * Yahata Shrine (Komukai Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Kanagawa Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Kanagawa Prefecture cultural property]], performed on the 2nd Sunday of August – ([[Saiwai Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) === September === * [[Kinza Hachimangū]] (Kinza Sasara Dance), designated as a [[Yokote City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15|1=September 15}} – ([[Yokote City]], {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}) === October === [[Image:熊野神社ささら獅子舞.jpg|代替文=Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai|サムネイル|Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai]] * Sugao Shrine (Hatsuyama Shishimai), designated as a Kanagawa cultural property, performed on the 1st Sunday of October – ([[Miyamae Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://takamizusan-sisimai.o.oo7.jp/ High Water Mountain Classical Lion Dance] * [http://nippara-forest.com/shishimai/index.html Shimonaguri Suwa Shrine Shishimai] * [http://purespirits.biz/sasara/ Nagano Shishimai of Kyuizu Shrine, Gyōda City, Saitama Prefecture] {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三匹獅子舞|version=101924445|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] {{wikidata link|Q11354485}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese folk culture]] [[Category:Japanese Lion Dance]] [[Category:Lions in art]] [[Category:Performing arts in the Edo period]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] '''Sanbiki Shishimai''' (三匹獅子舞) is a form of {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Lion dance|1=Lion dance}} performed by a trio of individuals, widely distributed throughout [[Eastern Japan]], particularly in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}. It is also referred to as ''Hitoridachi Sanbiki Shishimai'' or ''Santō Shishimai''. The performance is accompanied by {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue|1=Shinobue}} (bamboo flutes) and [[:en:Sasara|Sasara]] (percussion instruments), while the performers play {{ill|Taiko|en|Taiko|WD=Q221769|lt=Taiko|1=Taiko}} drums tied to their waists as they dance. Some performances include additional roles such as {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu|1=Tengu}}, {{ill|Kappa|en|Kappa|WD=Q14401|lt=Kappa|1=Kappa}}, [[:en:monkeys|monkeys]], ''tayū'' (masters), [[Shinto priests]], or even [[:en:nakadachi|nakadachi]] (intermediaries) serving as comedic characters. Primarily conducted during {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} [[:en:Festivals|Festivals]], the dances are offerings for [[bountiful harvests]], {{ill|disaster prevention|en|disaster prevention|WD=Q30899704|lt=disaster prevention|1=Disaster prevention}}, [[rain prayers]], and gratitude. Locals often refer to the dance as "shishi," "shishimai," "sasara shishimai," or simply "sasara." This style of lion dance belongs to the ''fūryū'' (aesthetic) tradition, which developed during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Middle Ages|1=Middle Ages}} and {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, distinct from [[gagaku]]-based lion dances of {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient history|1=Ancient history}} and [[Kagura]] origin. {{右|[[Image:San002.JPG|thumb|240px|none|Togasaki Katori Shrine
Sanbiki Shishimai performance
(Photographed on July 6, 2003)]]}} == Current Preservation == Unlike large shrine festivals supported by urban {{ill|Ujiko|en|Ujiko|WD=Q125562175|lt=Ujiko|1=Ujiko}} (parishioners), most Sanbiki Shishimai performances are small-scale "village festivals" with limited recognition outside the local community. Many groups traditionally limited the transmission of the dance to the [[eldest sons]] of farming families, ensuring continuity by choosing individuals unlikely to leave their land. However, adherence to this practice in modern times has caused the dance to fade in some areas. Conversely, some groups now teach the tradition to local {{ill|elementary|en|elementary|WD=Q402137|lt=elementary|1=Elementary}} and [[:en:junior high school|junior high school]] students. == Performance Locations and Dates == === January === * {{ill|Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja|銚子口香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chōshiguchi Shishimai), designated as a [[Kasukabe City]] cultural property, performed on the Sunday closest to {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15|1=January 15}} – ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} [[Kasukabe City]]) === March === * Aizu Higan Shishi (Komatsu Shishimai of Kita-Aizu), heralding spring in {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu|1=Aizu}} during the {{ill|Equinox|en|Equinox|WD=Q1315|lt=Equinox|1=Equinox}}. Notably, during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, [[Yamakawa Ōkura]] of the {{ill|Aizu Domain|en|Aizu Domain|WD=Q4389944|lt=Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain}} led the dance to [[:en:Wakamatsu Castle|Wakamatsu Castle]] as it was under siege – ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|en|Aizuwakamatsu|WD=Q237699|lt=Aizuwakamatsu|1=Aizuwakamatsu}}) === April === * {{ill|Gwaneumsa|en|Gwaneumsa|WD=Q840756|lt=Kannonji (Ishihara Sasara Shishimai)|1=Gwaneumsa}} – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Saitama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], held on the 3rd weekend of April – ([[Kawagoe City]], Saitama) * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} (Higashikogura Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Nikkō City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}} ({{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Shōwa Day|1=Shōwa Day}}) – ([[Nikkō City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}) === May === * {{ill|Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|ja|鳥見神社 (印西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|1=Torimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675121}} (Torimi Shrine Shishimai), designated as a [[Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Chiba Prefecture cultural property]], performed on {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}} – ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, [[Inzai City]]) * Nagasaki Shrine (Shishimai of Nagasaki Shrine) – ([[Toshima City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}) === July === [[Image:Tabayama Sasara-Jishi2.JPG|240px|thumb|right|[[Tabayama Gion Festival]]
Sasara Shishimai performance
(Photographed July 10, 2010)]] * Ose Sengen and Hikawa Shrines (Ose Shishimai) – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], performed on the first weekend of July – ([[Yashio City]], Saitama) === August === * Yahata Shrine (Komukai Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Kanagawa Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Kanagawa Prefecture cultural property]], performed on the 2nd Sunday of August – ([[Saiwai Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) === September === * [[Kinza Hachimangū]] (Kinza Sasara Dance), designated as a [[Yokote City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15|1=September 15}} – ([[Yokote City]], {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}) === October === [[Image:熊野神社ささら獅子舞.jpg|代替文=Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai|サムネイル|Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai]] * Sugao Shrine (Hatsuyama Shishimai), designated as a Kanagawa cultural property, performed on the 1st Sunday of October – ([[Miyamae Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://takamizusan-sisimai.o.oo7.jp/ High Water Mountain Classical Lion Dance] * [http://nippara-forest.com/shishimai/index.html Shimonaguri Suwa Shrine Shishimai] * [http://purespirits.biz/sasara/ Nagano Shishimai of Kyuizu Shrine, Gyōda City, Saitama Prefecture] {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三匹獅子舞|version=101924445|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三匹獅子舞]] {{wikidata link|Q11354485}} [[Category:Japanese folk culture]] [[Category:Japanese Lion Dance]] [[Category:Lions in art]] [[Category:Performing arts in the Edo period]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

However, adherence to this practice in modern times has caused the dance to fade in some areas. Conversely, some groups now teach the tradition to local {{ill|elementary|en|elementary|WD=Q402137|lt=elementary|1=Elementary}} and [[:en:junior high school|junior high school]] students. == Performance Locations and Dates == === January === * {{ill|Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja|銚子口香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshiguchi Katori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chōshiguchi Shishimai), designated as a [[Kasukabe City]] cultural property, performed on the Sunday closest to {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15|1=January 15}} – ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} [[Kasukabe City]]) === March === * Aizu Higan Shishi (Komatsu Shishimai of Kita-Aizu), heralding spring in {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu|1=Aizu}} during the {{ill|Equinox|en|Equinox|WD=Q1315|lt=Equinox|1=Equinox}}. Notably, during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, [[Yamakawa Ōkura]] of the {{ill|Aizu Domain|en|Aizu Domain|WD=Q4389944|lt=Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain}} led the dance to [[:en:Wakamatsu Castle|Wakamatsu Castle]] as it was under siege – ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|en|Aizuwakamatsu|WD=Q237699|lt=Aizuwakamatsu|1=Aizuwakamatsu}}) === April === * {{ill|Gwaneumsa|en|Gwaneumsa|WD=Q840756|lt=Kannonji (Ishihara Sasara Shishimai)|1=Gwaneumsa}} – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Saitama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], held on the 3rd weekend of April – ([[Kawagoe City]], Saitama) * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} (Higashikogura Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Nikkō City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}} ({{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Shōwa Day|1=Shōwa Day}}) – ([[Nikkō City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}) === May === * {{ill|Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|ja|鳥見神社 (印西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shrine (Inzai)|1=Torimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675121}} (Torimi Shrine Shishimai), designated as a [[Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties#Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Chiba Prefecture cultural property]], performed on {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}} – ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, [[Inzai City]]) * Nagasaki Shrine (Shishimai of Nagasaki Shrine) – ([[Toshima City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}) === July === [[Image:Tabayama Sasara-Jishi2.JPG|240px|thumb|right|[[Tabayama Gion Festival]]
Sasara Shishimai performance
(Photographed July 10, 2010)]] * Ose Sengen and Hikawa Shrines (Ose Shishimai) – [[Saitama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property]], performed on the first weekend of July – ([[Yashio City]], Saitama) === August === * Yahata Shrine (Komukai Shishimai) – Designated as a [[Kanagawa Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Kanagawa Prefecture cultural property]], performed on the 2nd Sunday of August – ([[Saiwai Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) === September === * [[Kinza Hachimangū]] (Kinza Sasara Dance), designated as a [[Yokote City]] cultural property, performed on {{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15|1=September 15}} – ([[Yokote City]], {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}) === October === [[Image:熊野神社ささら獅子舞.jpg|代替文=Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai|サムネイル|Kumano Shrine Sasara Shishimai]] * Sugao Shrine (Hatsuyama Shishimai), designated as a Kanagawa cultural property, performed on the 1st Sunday of October – ([[Miyamae Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]) == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://takamizusan-sisimai.o.oo7.jp/ High Water Mountain Classical Lion Dance] * [http://nippara-forest.com/shishimai/index.html Shimonaguri Suwa Shrine Shishimai] * [http://purespirits.biz/sasara/ Nagano Shishimai of Kyuizu Shrine, Gyōda City, Saitama Prefecture] {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三匹獅子舞|version=101924445|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11354485|ja|三匹獅子舞}} [[Category:Japanese folk culture]] [[Category:Japanese Lion Dance]] [[Category:Lions in art]] [[Category:Performing arts in the Edo period]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
